internet_facing_services: Online banking portal (HTTPS, Nginx + Tomcat, port 443)
ATM network management interface (VLAN-isolated, Internet-routable backup link)
supply_chain_vendors: Temenos (core banking SaaS support, VPN access)
critical_data_types: Customer PII (names, national ID, CIN, passports)
Account and transaction records (~2.1 million accounts)

regulatory_frameworks: GDPR (partial — EU operations via Paris branch)
certifications: ISO 27001:2022 (certified, scope: core banking and data centre)
data_residency_requirements: Customer PII must remain within Morocco (Law 09-08)
SWIFT messages: Morocco primary, OVHcloud EU for DR only
Card data: Worldline EU datacentre (contractual obligation)

notable_partnerships: Correspondent banking: BNP Paribas, Société Générale, CIB Egypt

crown_jewels: Temenos T24 core banking database (Oracle 19c, account balances and transactions)
SWIFT Alliance Access infrastructure (interbank transfer authorisation)
CyberArk vault (privileged credentials for 340 admin accounts)
Loan origination models and credit scoring algorithms
previous_incidents: 2022-Q3: Phishing campaign targeting teller workstations; 3 credentials compromised, contained before lateral movement
2023-Q1: Ransomware pre-positioning discovered on Citrix server (LockBit affiliate TTPs); isolated and remediated in 18h
2023-Q4: Business Email Compromise attempt on CFO; wire transfer of MAD 4.2M intercepted by dual-approval control
2024-Q2: DDoS against online banking portal (peak 38 Gbps); mitigated via Cloudflare Magic Transit (emergency contract)
2024-Q4: Supply-chain compromise of Atos contractor laptop; malware detected by CrowdStrike before network propagation
threat_concerns: Ransomware targeting North African financial institutions (LockBit 3.0, ALPHV/BlackCat successors, Medusa)
Nation-state APTs with North Africa nexus (APT43, Charming Kitten — financial sector espionage)
Insider threat from third-party IT contractors (Atos, Temenos support)
SWIFT fraud (Business Email Compromise + SWIFT operator targeting)
ATM jackpotting and logical attacks (Diebold Nixdorf firmware vulnerabilities)
Supply-chain attacks through core banking vendor update channels
Mobile banking API abuse (credential stuffing, ATO — 600+ failed logins/day average)
Zero-day exploitation of internet-facing Citrix and Nginx infrastructure
Data exfiltration targeting customer PII for sale on dark web forums
